existing_capabilities: ORP Pacing Engine (competitive core — do not rebuild)
Gesture System v4 (calibrated swipes, WPM dial, hints)
Basic App Scaffold (library, reading, parallax_reading, settings, analytics screens)
assumptions: Isar is the persistence layer for new features; existing SharedPreferences config stays
Desktop/web targets are stretch goals, not blockers for any release
pdfrx FFI constraint limits PDF page rendering to main isolate; text classification can use Isolate.run()
Solo developer capacity: 20 hours/week, max 3 concurrent agents
Test baseline: 264 pass, 20 skip, 4 pre-existing failures — do not introduce new failures
Analysis baseline: dart analyze lib/ must report zero issues

releases.title: RunThru MVP

model.rationale: Release-level strategic planning requires broad reasoning

milestones.title: Identity and Infrastructure

epics.title: Rebrand
epics.description: Rename app to RunThru, update icons, store listing prep, remove all legacy app name references
guardrails: No ableist language in any copy or code comments

features.title: Code Reference Rename

stories.title: Remove all legacy app name references from Dart source

tasks.title: Rename string literals and comments in lib/
acceptance_criteria: Zero occurrences of legacy app name variants in lib/**/*.dart (excluding .g.dart) — grep pattern: 'speedy.boy'
All user-visible strings reference 'RunThru'
dart analyze lib/ reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: mechanical grep-and-replace across lib/**/*.dart — single operation verifiable by grep returning zero matches

tasks.title: Rename string literals and comments in test/
acceptance_criteria: Zero occurrences of legacy app name variants in test/**/*.dart — grep pattern: 'speedy.boy'
flutter test completes with no new failures
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: mechanical grep-and-replace across test/**/*.dart — mirrors T1.1.1.1.1.1 in different directory scope, parallelizable

tasks.title: Update pubspec.yaml app name and description
acceptance_criteria: pubspec.yaml name field is 'runthru'
pubspec.yaml description references RunThru, not the legacy app name
flutter pub get succeeds
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: single-file config edit (pubspec.yaml name and description fields) — 15-minute change not worth subdividing

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 3 tasks across lib/, test/, and pubspec.yaml — same user outcome (no legacy app name references) but different file scopes enable parallel execution

stories.title: Remove all legacy app name references from platform configs

tasks.title: Update Android app name and package references
acceptance_criteria: Android app_name is 'RunThru'
Zero matches for legacy app name pattern in android/ directory — grep pattern: 'speedy'
flutter build apk --debug completes without errors
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: Android platform config files (manifest, build.gradle, strings.xml, settings.gradle) change together as one platform unit

tasks.title: Update iOS app name and bundle references
acceptance_criteria: iOS CFBundleDisplayName is 'RunThru'
Zero matches for legacy app name pattern in ios/ directory — grep pattern: 'speedy'
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: iOS platform config files (Info.plist, project.pbxproj) change together as one platform unit

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 platform config tasks (Android, iOS) — same outcome (platform names updated) but platform-isolated files enable parallel execution

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: code reference renaming is mechanical grep-and-replace across Dart/test files — distinct tooling and skill from visual asset replacement in F1.1.1.2

features.title: App Icon and Splash Screen

stories.title: Replace app icons with RunThru branding

tasks.title: Generate and place RunThru launcher icons for Android and iOS

model.rationale: Asset pipeline configuration requires understanding platform conventions

acceptance_criteria: Launcher icons exist at all required Android densities (mdpi through xxxhdpi)
iOS AppIcon.appiconset contains all required sizes per Contents.json
Icons do not reference the legacy app name visually or in filenames

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: icon replacement is one design-then-place operation across both platforms — not worth splitting per-platform since assets are generated together

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: app icons require platform-specific asset pipelines (Android mipmap densities, iOS xcassets) — different skill from text find-replace

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: rebrand is a cross-cutting concern spanning Dart source, test files, platform configs (Android XML, iOS plist), asset files, and pubspec.yaml — requires coordinated multi-file sweep

epics.title: CI/CD Foundation
epics.description: GitHub Actions lint+test pipeline, Fastlane signing scaffolding, release build verification

model.rationale: Infrastructure coordination across multiple config files

guardrails: CI must not allow test count regression

features.title: GitHub Actions Pipeline

stories.title: Create lint and test workflow

tasks.title: Create GitHub Actions workflow for analyze + test on PR

model.rationale: YAML workflow requires understanding CI patterns

acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists and is valid YAML
Workflow triggers on push to main and pull_request
Workflow runs 'flutter pub get', 'dart analyze --fatal-infos', 'flutter test'
Workflow uses Flutter stable channel

tasks.title: Create release build verification workflow
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/release-build.yml exists and is valid YAML
Workflow runs 'flutter build apk --release' successfully
Workflow triggers on push to main with version tag pattern
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: single YAML workflow file (.github/workflows/release-build.yml) — independent trigger pattern from ci.yml, parallelizable

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 CI workflow tasks (lint+test, release build) — independent YAML files with no shared state, parallelizable by two agents

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: GitHub Actions uses YAML workflow syntax on Ubuntu runners — distinct from Fastlane Ruby DSL in F1.1.2.2

features.title: Fastlane Signing Scaffolding

tasks.title: Create Fastlane configuration files for iOS and Android

acceptance_criteria: ios/fastlane/Fastfile exists with beta and release lanes
android/fastlane/Fastfile exists with beta and release lanes
Appfile references correct bundle IDs for RunThru
No signing secrets hardcoded in any file (uses environment variables)

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: Fastlane setup for both platforms is one coordinated configuration — splitting iOS/Android would create cross-dependency on shared signing conventions

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: Fastlane involves platform signing certificates, provisioning profiles, and Ruby lane definitions — isolated from GitHub Actions YAML

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: CI/CD infrastructure (GitHub Actions YAML, Fastlane Ruby DSL) is a distinct skill domain from app Dart code — parallelizable with rebrand due to zero file overlap

parallel_clusters.rationale: Rebrand and CI/CD have zero file overlap — fully independent

milestones.granularity_rationale: Milestone boundary: identity and CI/CD must stabilize before feature branches begin — all subsequent milestones depend on correct app naming and automated build verification

milestones.title: Calming UI + Design System

model.rationale: Milestone planning with design system and state migration coordination

epics.title: Design System
epics.description: Warm grays, soft creams, muted sage/slate; Atkinson Hyperlegible default, OpenDyslexic option; CVD-safe blue+orange; 3-4 themes (Light Warm, Dark, Sepia, High Contrast); Flutter ThemeData tokens

model.rationale: Design system requires coordinating color science, accessibility, and Flutter theming APIs

features.title: Color Token System

stories.title: Define semantic color tokens for all themes

tasks.title: Create RunThruColors token class with Light Warm, Dark, Sepia, High Contrast palettes

model.rationale: Color system design requires understanding WCAG contrast and CVD simulation

acceptance_criteria: RunThruColors class defines semantic tokens (surface, onSurface, primary, secondary, accent, error) for 4 themes
All color pairs meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio (≥4.5:1 for text, ≥3:1 for large text)
Blue+orange CVD-safe pair with lightness differentiation verified against protanopia simulation
No raw Color(0xFF...) values in any widget — all access via RunThruColors
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: color token class is one Dart file defining all 4 theme palettes — splitting per-theme would scatter semantically related constants across files

tasks.title: Create ThemeData factories for each theme variant
acceptance_criteria: RunThruTheme class provides static ThemeData factories: lightWarm(), dark(), sepia(), highContrast()
Each ThemeData uses RunThruColors tokens — no hardcoded colors
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: ThemeData factory is one Dart file consuming color tokens — tightly coupled to T1.2.1.1.1.1 token API but parallelizable via agreed interface contract

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: color token definition and ThemeData factory — tightly coupled by API contract but parallelizable via agreed token interface

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: color tokens are the foundation consumed by typography, decoration, and animation tokens — must stabilize API surface before dependents begin

features.title: Typography Token System

stories.title: Define typography tokens with Atkinson Hyperlegible and OpenDyslexic

tasks.title: Create RunThruTypography token class with font family switching

model.rationale: Typography architecture with accessibility font support

acceptance_criteria: RunThruTypography provides semantic text styles (headline, body, caption, label) via factory methods
Atkinson Hyperlegible is the default font family
OpenDyslexic is available as an accessibility option (NEVER paywalled)
Font family switching does not require app restart
No hardcoded TextStyle in any widget — all via RunThruTypography
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: typography token class is one Dart file with font-family switching — splitting per-style-variant would break the cohesive TextTheme API

tasks.title: Bundle Atkinson Hyperlegible and OpenDyslexic font assets
acceptance_criteria: Atkinson Hyperlegible font files (Regular, Bold, Italic, BoldItalic) exist in assets/fonts/
OpenDyslexic font files exist in assets/fonts/
pubspec.yaml declares both font families under flutter.fonts
flutter pub get succeeds
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: font asset placement is one mechanical copy-and-declare operation — Haiku-routed due to zero decision complexity

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: typography Dart code and font asset bundling — different file types (.dart vs .ttf + pubspec.yaml) enable parallel execution

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: typography tokens require font asset bundling (pubspec.yaml fonts section, OTF/TTF files) plus family-switching logic — different concern from color math

features.title: Decoration and Animation Tokens

stories.title: Define decoration factories and animation constants

tasks.title: Create RunThruDecorations factory with neumorphic and elevation styles

model.rationale: Decoration system requires understanding shadow/elevation patterns

acceptance_criteria: RunThruDecorations class provides factory methods for card, elevated, inset, and neumorphic styles
All shadows use theme-aware colors from RunThruColors
No hardcoded BoxDecoration shadows in any widget
Rounded geometry: default borderRadius ≥12.0
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: decoration factory is one Dart file defining shadow/border/radius recipes — depends on color tokens being defined, single-file output

tasks.title: Create RunThruAnimations constants with reduced motion support
acceptance_criteria: RunThruAnimations provides duration and curve constants for standard, slow-eased, and spring animations
Every animation constant has a reduced-motion variant (shorter duration or no animation)
isReducedMotion(context) helper function is available
Text fades use slow-eased curves (≥300ms default duration)
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: animation constants is one Dart file defining duration/curve pairs with reduced-motion variants — independent from decoration factory file

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: decoration factories and animation constants — independent design token categories sharing only color token dependency

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: decoration factories and animation constants depend on color tokens being defined — separate rendering concern from text styling, parallelizable internally

ethical_blockers.rule: Accessibility features (OpenDyslexic font, CVD-safe themes, font size controls) are NEVER paywalled — free forever per ethical commitment

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: design system defines color, typography, decoration, and animation tokens consumed by every widget — requires CVD validation, WCAG contrast math, and Flutter ThemeData coordination across 3 features

epics.title: Calming UI Application
epics.description: Apply design system to all screens; generous whitespace, rounded geometry, slow-eased transitions; text fades softly; CVD simulator pass
guardrails: CVD simulator pass required before merge

All colors from RunThruColors tokens
All text styles from RunThruTypography tokens
Generous whitespace (minimum 16dp padding between sections)
Rounded geometry on all cards (borderRadius ≥12)
Touch targets ≥48dp on all interactive elements

tasks.title: Refactor settings_screen.dart to use design tokens and ConsumerWidget
acceptance_criteria: settings_screen.dart uses ConsumerWidget (no StatefulWidget)
All colors from RunThruColors tokens

Theme selector allows switching between Light Warm, Dark, Sepia, High Contrast
Font selector allows switching between Atkinson Hyperlegible and OpenDyslexic
Touch targets ≥48dp on all interactive elements

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 screen redesign tasks (library, settings) — independent screen files with no shared state enable parallel execution by two agents

Text transitions use slow-eased fades (≥300ms)

acceptance_criteria: analytics_screen.dart uses ConsumerWidget
All colors from RunThruColors tokens

Touch targets ≥48dp

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 screen redesign tasks (reading, analytics) — independent screen files enable parallel execution despite different animation complexity

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: reading and analytics screens have different animation requirements — reading needs slow-eased word fades, analytics needs data visualization patterns

features.title: CVD Simulator Verification

stories.title: Verify all themes pass CVD simulation

tasks.title: Create contrast audit test covering all theme variants

model.rationale: Test creation requires understanding color science

acceptance_criteria: Test file verifies WCAG 4.5:1 contrast for all text color pairs in all 4 themes
Test file verifies WCAG 3:1 contrast for all large text and UI component pairs
Test passes for protanopia, deuteranopia, and tritanopia simulated palettes
All tests pass with zero failures
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: contrast audit is one test file asserting WCAG ratios across all 4 theme × color-pair combinations — single verification artifact

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: contrast audit is one comprehensive test file covering all theme × color-pair combinations — one test suite, one verification command

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: CVD verification is a test-only pass gated on all screen redesigns completing — produces no production code, only test assertions against color pairs

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: applying design system requires touching every existing screen file — depends on both E1.2.1 (design tokens) and E1.2.3 (ConsumerWidget migration) completing first

epics.title: Riverpod State Migration
epics.description: Migrate existing StatefulWidget screens to ConsumerWidget; establish provider architecture. FOUNDATIONAL — other epics depend on it.

model.rationale: Foundational state architecture migration affecting all screens

instruction_file_updates: Append to CLAUDE.md ## State Management — Riverpod Conventions: providers co-located with features, auto-dispose by default, codegen with @riverpod annotation

features.title: Provider Architecture Foundation

stories.title: Establish feature-scoped provider structure

Provider uses AutoDisposeNotifier pattern

lib/features/settings/providers/ directory exists with theme and font providers

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 provider scaffolding tasks (reading feature, library+settings feature) — independent feature directories enable parallel agent execution

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: provider architecture scaffolding creates directory structure and exemplar providers — must exist before screen migration (F1.2.3.2) can reference providers

stories.title: Migrate StatefulWidget screens to ConsumerWidget

acceptance_criteria: library_screen.dart extends ConsumerWidget (not StatefulWidget)
No setState() calls remain in the file
All shared state managed via Riverpod providers
Existing functionality preserved (no regressions in integration tests)

tasks.title: Migrate settings_screen.dart from StatefulWidget to ConsumerWidget
acceptance_criteria: settings_screen.dart extends ConsumerWidget (not StatefulWidget)
No setState() calls remain
Theme and font preferences stored via Riverpod provider backed by SharedPreferences/Isar
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: settings_screen.dart migration is one file converting StatefulWidget → ConsumerWidget — parallelizable with library and analytics

tasks.title: Migrate analytics_screen.dart from StatefulWidget to ConsumerWidget
acceptance_criteria: analytics_screen.dart extends ConsumerWidget (not StatefulWidget)
No setState() calls remain
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: analytics_screen.dart migration is one file converting StatefulWidget → ConsumerWidget — parallelizable with library and settings

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 3 screen migration tasks — independent screen files enable 3-agent parallel execution at maximum concurrent capacity

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: screen migration to ConsumerWidget is mechanical once providers exist — three independent screen files parallelizable at 3-agent capacity

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: Riverpod state migration is foundational architecture — 8 downstream epics declare depends_on E1.2.3 because they require ConsumerWidget + provider patterns established

parallel_clusters.rationale: Design system tokens and Riverpod migration touch different files — can proceed in parallel
Three screen migrations are independent after provider scaffolding is done

milestones.granularity_rationale: Milestone boundary: design system tokens and Riverpod provider architecture are consumed by every downstream screen epic — 8 epics declare dependency on M1.2 outputs

milestones.title: Content Ingestion

epics.title: Universal Share Sheet

model.rationale: Platform-native integration across iOS and Android requires coordinated planning

guardrails: Privacy: no content uploaded to cloud

AndroidManifest.xml declares intent-filter for ACTION_SEND with text/html MIME type
AndroidManifest.xml declares intent-filter for ACTION_VIEW with http/https schemes
flutter build apk --debug completes without errors

tasks.title: Create Dart-side intent handler provider to receive and route shared content

acceptance_criteria: ShareIntentProvider exists as @riverpod annotated provider
SharedContent model class distinguishes text, URL, and file types
Provider handles incoming intent on app cold start and while running
Content stored on-device only (no cloud upload)
dart analyze reports zero issues

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: Android manifest config and Dart intent handler — tightly coupled platform channel pair but touch different file types (XML vs Dart)

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: Android Intent receiver uses AndroidManifest.xml intent-filters and Dart platform channels — platform-isolated from iOS Share Extension in F1.3.1.2

App Group configured for data sharing between extension and main app

tasks.title: Create Dart-side handler to read content from iOS app group on launch
acceptance_criteria: ShareIntentProvider handles iOS app group content on app foreground
Shared content from iOS extension appears in library within 1 second of app open
Content stored on-device only
dart analyze reports zero issues

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: Xcode target creation then Dart-side reader — sequential dependency (Swift extension must exist before Dart handler can read App Group)

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: iOS Share Extension requires Xcode target creation, App Group configuration, and Swift code — platform-isolated from Android intent handling

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: universal share sheet coordinates iOS Share Extension (Swift, Xcode target, App Group) with Android Intent receiver (manifest XML, Dart handler) — two platform-native implementations sharing one Dart provider

epics.title: Content Pipeline Cleanup
epics.description: PDF cleanup with pdfrx, EPUB cleanup, plain text/Markdown/HTML normalisation; beta label OK

features.title: PDF Extraction Cleanup

stories.title: Stabilize PDF text extraction pipeline

tasks.title: Refactor pdf_extractor.dart to handle edge cases and add progress reporting
acceptance_criteria: PDF extraction reports progress as percentage (0-100) via stream
Handles PDFs with no text layer gracefully (returns empty with informative message)
Classification logic runs in Isolate.run(), not main isolate
pdfrx page rendering stays on main isolate (FFI constraint)
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: pdf_extractor.dart refactoring adds progress stream and error handling to one existing file — splitting would create merge conflicts in shared extraction function

tasks.title: Add unit tests for PDF extraction edge cases

model.rationale: Test creation for complex extraction logic

acceptance_criteria: Tests cover: empty PDF, single page, multi-page, PDF with no text layer
Tests verify progress reporting emits values 0-100
Tests verify classification runs off main isolate (via mock verification)
All tests pass
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: PDF extraction test file covers all edge cases in one test suite — parallelizable with implementation refactor via expected API contract

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: PDF refactoring and its test file — implementation and verification parallelizable since tests can be written against expected API contract

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: PDF extraction has unique FFI constraint (pdfrx on main isolate) absent from EPUB/text processing — requires different concurrency strategy

acceptance_criteria: EPUB extraction detects chapter boundaries and reports them as sections
Progress reporting via stream (0-100%)
HTML stripping runs in Isolate.run()
Handles malformed EPUB gracefully (informative error, no crash)

tasks.title: Add unit tests for EPUB extraction
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover: valid EPUB, EPUB with no chapters, malformed EPUB
Tests verify chapter boundary detection
All tests pass
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: EPUB extraction test file covers edge cases in one test suite — parallelizable with implementation refactor

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: EPUB refactoring and its test file — mirrors PDF story pattern, implementation and verification parallelizable

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: EPUB parsing uses dart_epub_viewer with HTML-stripping pipeline — different library, processing model, and chapter-detection logic from PDF or plain text

HTML tags stripped; Markdown formatting stripped
Paragraph boundaries preserved as section markers
Processing runs in Isolate.run() for inputs >10KB

tasks.title: Add unit tests for content normaliser
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover: plain text, Markdown with headings/lists/emphasis, HTML with divs/spans/links
Tests verify paragraph boundary preservation
Tests verify large input (>10KB) processes without blocking main isolate

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: content pipeline cleanup spans three extraction formats (PDF, EPUB, text/HTML) with shared isolate concurrency patterns — sequenced after E1.3.1 so content routing is established

Content stored on-device only
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: file picker provider is one file wiring file_picker package to content routing — single integration point, not decomposable

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: file picker integration is a single provider wiring file_picker package output to content routing — not decomposable further

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: local file picker uses file_picker package with MIME type filtering — different platform bridge and user flow from clipboard detection

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: clipboard detection and file picker are independent content-ingestion sources with different platform APIs — parallelizable with E1.3.1 due to zero file overlap

parallel_clusters.rationale: Share sheet and clipboard/file picker are independent content sources with no file overlap
PDF refactor and its tests can be written in parallel

milestones.granularity_rationale: Milestone boundary: content ingestion is the primary user-acquisition funnel — users cannot experience reading UX without importable content, making this prerequisite to M1.4

milestones.title: Reading Experience Polish

model.rationale: Milestone planning for core reading UX

epics.title: RSVP UX Polish
epics.description: Warm-up ramp 80%→100% over ~10s, tap pause/resume, swipe-left rewind, smooth mode transitions

model.rationale: Core reading UX requires coordinating animation, gesture, and state

guardrails: ORP engine is existing — do not rebuild

features.title: Warm-up Ramp

stories.title: Implement gradual WPM warm-up at session start

tasks.title: Add warm-up ramp logic to word_timer.dart (80% → 100% over ~10s)
acceptance_criteria: Session starts at 80% of target WPM
WPM ramps linearly to 100% over configurable duration (default 10 seconds)
Ramp resets on session restart (double-tap)
Ramp can be disabled in settings
Existing word_timer_test.dart still passes
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: warm-up ramp adds timing curve calculation to word_timer.dart — modifies one existing file, verifiable by existing test suite plus new tests

tasks.title: Add unit tests for warm-up ramp behavior
acceptance_criteria: Test verifies first word displays at 80% of target WPM interval
Test verifies WPM reaches 100% after ramp duration
Test verifies ramp reset on restart
All tests pass
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: warm-up ramp tests extend existing word_timer_test.dart — parallelizable with implementation via behavior contract

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: timer logic implementation and its tests — implementation and test writing parallelizable since test can target expected behavior contract

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: warm-up ramp modifies word_timer.dart timing curve only — isolated from gesture handling code in tap/swipe feature F1.4.1.2

features.title: Tap Pause/Resume and Swipe Rewind

stories.title: Polish tap and swipe gestures for reading control

tasks.title: Ensure tap pause/resume is responsive with visual feedback
acceptance_criteria: Single tap toggles pause/resume within 100ms response time
Visual pause indicator appears (not color-only — uses icon + label)
Pause state accessible to screen reader (semantic label 'Paused' / 'Reading')
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: tap pause visual feedback modifies reading_screen.dart — one screen file change adding icon + semantic label for pause state

tasks.title: Implement swipe-left rewind with sentence boundary snapping
acceptance_criteria: Swipe-left rewinds to start of current sentence (via sentence_resolver)
If already at sentence start, rewinds to previous sentence
Swipe threshold uses calibrated value (30% screen width + velocity)
Rewind position preserved across pause/resume
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: swipe rewind modifies gesture_classifier.dart and sentence_resolver.dart — tightly coupled files that change together for rewind logic

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: tap pause visual feedback and swipe rewind logic — different gesture types modifying different code paths enable parallel execution

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: tap pause/resume and swipe rewind modify gesture_classifier.dart — isolated from timer arithmetic in warm-up ramp F1.4.1.1

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: RSVP UX polish modifies word timer timing, gesture classifier thresholds, and reading screen rendering — coordinates animation, gesture, and state concerns across 3 core files

epics.title: Multi-Mode Reading
epics.description: Sentence mode with highlight + auto-scroll, paragraph mode with auto-advance, seamless switching via gesture

model.rationale: New reading modes require coordinated state and UI architecture

features.title: Sentence Mode

stories.title: Implement sentence-at-a-time reading with word highlighting

tasks.title: Create sentence mode widget with current-word highlight and auto-scroll

model.rationale: Complex widget with animation and scroll coordination

acceptance_criteria: Displays full sentence with current word highlighted (bold + underline, not color-only)
Auto-scrolls to keep current word centered when sentence exceeds viewport
Highlight animation uses slow-eased transition (≥200ms)
Checks isReducedMotion(context) and disables animation if true
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: sentence mode is one new widget file combining highlight, auto-scroll, and animation — 4-hour estimate reflects animation complexity, not decomposable

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: sentence mode widget is one cohesive UI component — highlight, scroll, and animation are tightly coupled within one widget file

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: sentence mode is a new widget with word-level highlighting and auto-scroll — independent rendering model and widget tree from paragraph mode

features.title: Paragraph Mode

stories.title: Implement paragraph-at-a-time reading with auto-advance

tasks.title: Create paragraph mode widget with timed auto-advance
acceptance_criteria: Displays full paragraph with comfortable line spacing (≥1.5)
Auto-advances to next paragraph after calculated reading time (based on word count and WPM)
Progress indicator shows time remaining (not color-only — uses position + label)
Tap pauses auto-advance timer
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: paragraph mode is one new widget file combining timed advance, progress indicator, and line spacing — independent rendering model from sentence mode

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: paragraph mode widget is one cohesive UI component — timer, progress, and layout are tightly coupled within one widget file

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: paragraph mode has timed auto-advance logic absent from sentence mode — requires different timer, progress indicator, and line-spacing strategy

features.title: Mode Switching

stories.title: Seamless switching between RSVP, sentence, and paragraph modes

tasks.title: Create mode switcher provider and gesture handler
acceptance_criteria: ReadingModeProvider tracks current mode (rsvp, sentence, paragraph)
Mode switch preserves current position in text
Transition between modes uses crossfade animation (respects reduced motion)
Mode switcher touch target ≥48dp
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: mode switcher combines one provider file and one widget file — tightly coupled pair that must be implemented together to avoid orphan state

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: mode switcher combines one provider and one widget in tight coordination — splitting would create circular dependency

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: mode switching is a coordination layer that depends on both sentence and paragraph mode views existing — cannot implement until F1.4.2.1 and F1.4.2.2 complete

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: multi-mode reading introduces two new widget trees (sentence view, paragraph view) plus mode-switching state — depends on E1.4.1 establishing gesture and timer patterns

epics.title: Accessibility Foundations
epics.description: Adaptive letter/word spacing, reading ruler/focus line, no color-only signals, shape/label/position fallbacks

features.title: Adaptive Spacing Controls

stories.title: User-adjustable letter and word spacing

tasks.title: Create adaptive spacing provider and settings UI
acceptance_criteria: SpacingProvider stores letter spacing (0.0-4.0) and word spacing (0.0-8.0)
Settings UI shows slider controls with current value labels
Spacing applies to all reading modes (RSVP, sentence, paragraph)
Feature is FREE (not behind any paywall check)
Persisted across app restarts via SharedPreferences
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: spacing provider is one file with slider state and SharedPreferences persistence — parallelizable with reading ruler overlay

tasks.title: Create reading ruler/focus line overlay
acceptance_criteria: Reading ruler overlays a tinted bar above and below current line
Ruler position tracks current reading position
Ruler can be toggled on/off from reading screen
Feature is FREE (not behind any paywall check)
Ruler uses semi-transparent overlay (not full opacity — maintains readability)
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: reading ruler is one overlay widget file — parallelizable with spacing provider, consumes spacing values via ref.watch

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: spacing settings provider and reading ruler overlay — independent UI surfaces (settings screen vs reading screen) enable parallel execution

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: adaptive spacing combines a settings provider (settings screen) with a reading ruler overlay (reading screen) — two distinct UI surfaces with independent file scope

ethical_blockers.rule: Accessibility features (adaptive letter/word spacing, reading ruler, focus line) are NEVER paywalled — free forever per ethical commitment

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: accessibility foundations span settings providers, reading overlays, and design tokens — parallelizable with E1.4.1 because file scopes do not overlap

epics.title: Artifact Suppression
epics.description: Long-press suppression during pause, range select, per-source profiles, auto-detect tables/figures/code

model.rationale: Content classification system requires isolate strategy

features.title: Artifact Detection Engine

stories.title: Auto-detect tables, figures, and code blocks in content

tasks.title: Create artifact classifier that detects non-prose content in Isolate.run()
acceptance_criteria: ArtifactClassifier identifies tables (tab-separated or pipe-delimited patterns)
Identifies code blocks (indentation patterns, common syntax markers)
Identifies figure references (Figure X, Fig. X patterns)
Classification runs in Isolate.run() — verified by function signature accepting SendPort or returning Future
Returns List<ArtifactRegion> with start/end indices and type
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: artifact classifier is one new Dart file with pattern-matching logic wrapped in Isolate.run() — pure function, testable without UI

tasks.title: Add unit tests for artifact classification
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover: text with tables, text with code blocks, text with figure refs, mixed content
Tests verify correct region boundaries (±2 characters tolerance)
All tests pass
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: classifier test file covers table/code/figure detection patterns — parallelizable with implementation via expected classification contract

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: artifact classifier implementation and its test file — implementation and verification parallelizable

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: artifact detection is pure classification logic running in Isolate.run() — no UI dependency, testable in complete isolation from suppression overlay

features.title: Suppression UI

stories.title: Long-press suppression and range selection during pause

tasks.title: Create long-press gesture handler for manual artifact suppression
acceptance_criteria: Long-press during pause activates suppression mode
User can select word range to suppress
Suppressed regions shown with strikethrough + muted opacity (not color-only)
Suppression persisted per-document
Touch targets ≥48dp on selection handles
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: suppression overlay combines gesture handler and visual feedback in one widget — tightly coupled long-press + range-select interaction, not decomposable

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: suppression overlay is one gesture-driven widget — long-press, range select, and visual feedback are tightly coupled in one component

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: suppression UI requires gesture handling (long-press, range select) and overlay rendering — depends on classification engine output format from F1.4.4.1

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: artifact suppression combines an isolate-based classification engine with a gesture-driven suppression UI overlay — depends on E1.3.2 content pipeline for standardized input format

parallel_clusters.rationale: RSVP polish and accessibility foundations touch different files and can proceed in parallel
Warm-up implementation and tests can be written in parallel

milestones.granularity_rationale: Milestone boundary: reading experience polish layers on top of M1.2 design system and M1.3 content pipeline — cannot start without styled ConsumerWidget screens and importable content

epics.title: Stats + Analytics Foundation
epics.description: Session time, words read, sessions completed; on-device only
guardrails: No cloud upload without per-session opt-in

features.title: Session Tracking Provider

stories.title: Track reading session metrics on-device

tasks.title: Create session stats provider with Isar persistence
acceptance_criteria: SessionStatsProvider tracks: session duration, words read, WPM average, completion percentage
ReadingSession model stored in Isar with timestamp and document reference
Data stored on-device only — no network calls
Provider auto-disposes when reading screen is left

tasks.title: Create analytics dashboard widget for stats screen
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows: total sessions, total words read, average session duration, streak count
All data indicators use shape/label/position (not color-only)
Uses RunThruColors and RunThruTypography tokens
Touch targets ≥48dp on any interactive stat elements
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: stats dashboard widget is one file rendering aggregated session data — parallelizable with provider creation via agreed data model

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: Isar stats provider (data layer) and dashboard widget (presentation) — independent layers enable parallel execution

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: session tracking combines Isar persistence (data layer model + provider) with dashboard widget (presentation layer) — both co-located in features/stats/

ethical_blockers.rule: All analytics data stored on-device only — no cloud upload without explicit per-session opt-in

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: stats foundation requires coordinated Isar schema design, Riverpod provider, and dashboard widget across the new features/stats/ directory

epics.title: App Store Presence
epics.description: Screenshots, description, ASO, privacy policy, TestFlight/Play Store internal testing
guardrails: Privacy policy must reflect on-device-only data
No dark patterns in store listing

ethical_blockers.rule: No ableist language in store listing or screenshots

features.title: Store Listing Content

stories.title: Create store listing assets and metadata

tasks.title: Write App Store and Play Store descriptions with ASO keywords
acceptance_criteria: App Store description ≤4000 characters with keyword density for 'paced reading', 'ADHD reading', 'focus reading'
Play Store short description ≤80 characters
Play Store full description ≤4000 characters
Zero ableist language (no 'suffering from', 'overcoming disability', 'normal reading')
Positioning as completion app, not speed-reading app
No manipulative urgency language
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: store descriptions are one copywriting deliverable per platform — parallelizable with privacy policy, no code dependency

tasks.title: Create privacy policy documenting on-device-only data handling
acceptance_criteria: Privacy policy states all reading content stays on device
Explicitly states no cloud upload without per-session opt-in
Lists what data is collected (analytics on-device only)
Complies with GDPR and CCPA minimum requirements
Written in plain language (no legal jargon without explanation)
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: privacy policy is one legal document reflecting on-device-only data handling — parallelizable with store descriptions

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: store descriptions and privacy policy — independent documents with no shared content enable parallel writing

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: store listing content is copywriting and legal documentation — no code changes, distinct from Fastlane CI/CD configuration in F1.5.2.2

features.title: Internal Testing Setup

stories.title: Configure TestFlight and Play Store internal testing tracks

acceptance_criteria: Fastlane has 'internal' lane that uploads to Play Store internal testing track
Lane uses supply action with track: 'internal'
No signing secrets hardcoded (uses environment variables)
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: Play Store Fastlane internal lane is one config addition to android/fastlane/Fastfile — parallelizable with iOS TestFlight config

tasks.title: Configure TestFlight upload via Fastlane
acceptance_criteria: Fastlane has 'beta' lane that uploads to TestFlight
Lane uses pilot action
No signing secrets hardcoded (uses environment variables)
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: TestFlight Fastlane beta lane is one config addition to ios/fastlane/Fastfile — parallelizable with Android Play Store config

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: Play Store and TestFlight Fastlane configs — platform-independent lane definitions enable parallel configuration

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: internal testing setup modifies Fastlane lane definitions for iOS and Android — platform-specific distribution pipeline configs

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: app store presence spans platform-specific Fastlane configs, legal/privacy documents, and marketing copy — parallelizable with E1.5.1 due to zero file overlap

parallel_clusters.rationale: Stats foundation and store presence have no file overlap
Android and iOS Fastlane configs are independent

milestones.granularity_rationale: Milestone boundary: launch readiness is the final validation gate — store listing requires feature-complete app for screenshots, and stats require completed reading sessions to measure
